senses.definition: the action of providing people with activities, performances, etc. to make them laugh or amuse them

examples.x: The festival featured various forms of entertainment, including music, dance, and art.
They organized entertainment for the guests during the wedding reception.
Streaming services have changed how people access entertainment.
The theme park offers entertainment for visitors of all ages.

senses.definition: things done in one's free time for pleasure or enjoyment

examples.x: The community center offers various recreation programs for all ages.
Going for a hike is a popular form of recreation in the summer.
Gardening has become a favorite recreation for her over the years.
The park provides a space for outdoor recreation like picnicking and playing sports.

senses.definition: a type of game in which a player does not speak and only uses movements or gestures to express the meaning of a word or phrase so that the other players can guess what word or phrase it is

examples.x: She chose a difficult word for charades, but her friends were determined to guess it.
The group had a lot of fun playing charades at the party.
She acted out a famous movie scene during charades, and everyone laughed.
He struggled to convey his word in charades but finally got it with a gesture.
They decided to play charades as a team-building activity during the retreat.

senses.definition: a picture on a cardboard that is cut into different pieces and one should fit them together in order for the picture to become whole again

examples.x: She spent the afternoon working on a challenging jigsaw puzzle with her friends.
He felt accomplished when he finally completed the intricate jigsaw puzzle.
The family gathered around the table to start a new jigsaw puzzle for the weekend.
She enjoys the relaxing process of finding the right pieces for her jigsaw puzzle.
He received a beautiful jigsaw puzzle as a birthday gift, featuring a scenic landscape.

senses.definition: someone with whom a child plays

examples.x: His favorite playmate always brought exciting new toys to share.
The children laughed and played tag with their playmates in the park.
His little sister often joined him and his playmate for imaginative play.

senses.definition: one of the set of 52 cards with unique symbols and numbers on one side, used in specific card games

examples.x: He shuffled the playing cards before dealing them out to everyone.
She had a favorite playing card that she always kept hidden for luck.
The playing cards featured beautiful illustrations of mythical creatures.
They played a few rounds of poker with a standard deck of playing cards.
The playing cards were worn out from years of use but still functional.

senses.definition: (always plural) one of the four sets in a pack of playing cards that is marked with a black design in the shape of a leaf with 3 connected balls on a small stem

examples.x: In bridge, the clubs suit is often considered the lowest ranking.
The game requires players to follow the lead suit, which is clubs in this round.

senses.definition: (plural) one of the four sets in a pack of playing cards that is characterized by a red diamond shape

examples.x: The queen of diamonds sparkled under the light as it was placed on the table.
She won the hand with a flush, her cards all showing diamonds.

senses.definition: (plural) one of the four sets in a pack of playing cards that is marked with a red heart shape
(plural) one of the four sets in a pack of playing cards that is marked with a black design in the shape of a pointed leaf with a small stem
one of the playing cards with only one symbol on it, which in most card games is considered the highest-ranking card

examples.x: She played an ace to secure her victory in the game.
The ace can be worth either one or eleven points in blackjack.
He was excited to draw an ace from the deck.
In poker, an ace is often a valuable card to hold.

senses.definition: the set of playing cards that a player is given during a game

examples.x: He had a strong hand with three aces and two kings.
She was excited to receive a full house in her hand.
The dealer shuffled the cards and dealt each player a hand.
After looking at his hand, he decided to raise the bet.
She was confident in her hand and called the opponent's bluff.

senses.definition: a game for two players who take a turn to put an X or O in nine provided squares, and the first player who manages to make a row, column, or diagonal of three Xs or Os in those squares wins

examples.x: They played a quick game of tic-tac-toe during their lunch break.
She won the tic-tac-toe game with a clever diagonal move.
He showed his younger sister how to play tic-tac-toe.
The teacher introduced tic-tac-toe as a fun way to practice strategy.
After three rounds of tic-tac-toe, they decided to switch to checkers.

senses.definition: an indoor game for two teams of players each trying to throw a ball with their hands to the opponent's goal

examples.x: Playing handball competitively demands both physical stamina and strategic thinking on the court.
She joined the school's handball club to improve her agility and coordination skills.
The championship match in handball drew a large crowd of enthusiastic spectators.
Learning the rules and tactics of handball helped him appreciate the sport's complexity and excitement.
She has been practicing handball for several years.

senses.definition: a type of doll that, based on popular belief, represents an attractive young woman

examples.x: She dressed her Barbie doll in a sparkly evening gown for the party.
He surprised her with a vintage Barbie doll from the 1980s.
The Barbie doll came with a set of mini accessories for her dream house.
The new Barbie doll set included a car and a pet dog.

senses.definition: each of a set of small flat blocks, made of wood, plastic, etc., with spots that represent numbers on one side, used in specific games

examples.x: The domino that was placed sideways was the starting piece.
He won the match by placing a double-six domino.

senses.definition: a human-like soft doll made from cloth pieces

examples.x: The little girl hugged her favorite rag doll tightly at bedtime.
They made a rag doll together during the craft class.
Her grandmother gifted her a handmade rag doll for her birthday.
The rag doll had colorful patches stitched all over it.
The rag doll was so worn out that its stitching began to unravel.

senses.definition: one of the small objects that a player moves around in board games

examples.x: She moved her game piece forward three spaces.
Each player picked a unique piece to use during the game.
He accidentally knocked over his game piece while reaching for the dice.

senses.definition: a type of toy that consists of colored plastic blocks that fit together and using which children can build different things

examples.x: She enjoyed playing with Lego more than any other toy.
The Lego on the floor created a colorful landscape of buildings.
He built a detailed robot using his Lego.
The Lego in the box was designed to create a famous landmark.

senses.definition: a type of board game in which players use game currency to buy buildings or streets represented on the board
a type of board game in which one tries to make words on a board using small blocks with letters on them
a board that displays the score in a contest or game

examples.x: The scoreboard displayed the team's points during the basketball game.
The scoreboard updated automatically after each round of play.
He glanced at the scoreboard to see how much time was left in the match.
The scoreboard lit up with fireworks when a player scored.
They took a picture of the scoreboard to remember the exciting finish.

senses.definition: an image that is the representation of a player in a game or an account on social media

examples.x: She created a colorful avatar to represent her in the online game.
His avatar in the virtual world looked just like him, with similar clothes and hairstyle.
Players can customize their avatar with different outfits and accessories.
The avatar danced around the screen during the interactive party.
She chose a cute animal as her avatar for the social media platform.

senses.definition: an upright lever that can be moved in different directions to control an image or character in a video game

examples.x: He used the joystick to navigate his character through the game.
She found the joystick easy to use, making the game more enjoyable.
The arcade was filled with machines that had bright joysticks for players to use.
He adjusted the joystick to aim at the target in the shooting game.
The game console came with a new joystick that offered better precision.
